chunk_summary: Guidelines for safe formula preparation, hygiene, and feeding techniques, including cup feeding, to prevent diarrhea, undernutrition, and other risks in infants aged 0–24 months.
learning_objectives: Understand safe preparation techniques for powdered infant formula, including water temperature and storage guidelines.
Learn hygienic feeding practices, including sterilization and proper cleaning of feeding equipment.
Recognize the importance of paced feeding and satiety cues to prevent overfeeding and aspiration risks.
Explore strategies to protect breastfeeding during mixed feeding and transition to cup drinking by the first year.
key_facts: Boil water and cool to ~70°C before mixing powdered formula to reduce bacterial contamination.
Prepared formula should be refrigerated immediately and discarded after 24 hours; avoid room temperature storage.
Over-dilution of formula can cause hyponatremia and poor weight gain, while over-concentration increases renal solute load.
Prefer cup/spoon feeding or paladai over bottles, especially in areas with poor sanitation.
Transition away from bottles to cup drinking by the end of the first year to reduce dental caries risk and support oral-motor development.
reference_quote: Use potable water; boil and cool to ~70°C before mixing powdered formula to reduce bacterial contamination. — World Health Organization
